title: slovakian kid-friendly poke
description: a simplified version of traditional slovakian dish featuring poached chicken, raw vegetables, and cultured crème fraîche. suitable for children.

ingredients.name: chicken breast
ingredients.notes: boneless, skinless, and cut into small pieces

instructions: in a large pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil.
add 1 tablespoon of salt and 1 tablespoon of black pepper.
reduce heat to a simmer and poach the chicken for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
remove the chicken from the water and let it cool.
in a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
add the flour and whisk until a smooth paste forms.
slowly add the milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until thickened.
remove from heat and stir in the cream cheese until melted.
stir in the cultured crème fraîche until well combined.
season the sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
in a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken, carrots, celery, onion, lettuce, and tomato.
pour the sauce over the top and toss to combine.
cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
just before serving, sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve chilled.
